review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control's intent to manage high-risk user access through RBAC in Kubernetes. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but a minor change was made for clarity. The functions map to real K8s security controls by focusing on RBAC management, which is appropriate for this control.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly specify the action being taken, such as 'access revocation' instead of just 'revocation policy'.
title: Account Management | Disable Accounts for High-risk Individuals
step1_reasoning: The control AC-2 (13) focuses on disabling accounts for high-risk individuals. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to RBAC by ensuring that high-risk users do not have access to sensitive roles or bindings. This involves disabling or revoking access for service accounts or users identified as high-risk, and implementing policies to regularly review and manage user access.
